title: Phase 5: Implement Design Token Compiler
description: Build compilation pipeline: frozen Pydantic models for tokens. Single-source enforcement (errors if both YAML + dict provided). compile_tokens(tokens, mode) for themed/minimal/headless. All color tokens emit light-dark() notation with color-scheme: light dark on :root. No @media prefers-color-scheme blocks, no .dark class, no a11y @media (genX handles). Heavily commented output for AI coding assistants. Auto-compile on startup (check freshness, recompile if stale). CSS delivery: get_css_path(mode), get_css_link_tag(mode), get_tablix_static_mount() for FastAPI.

title: Phase 3: Implement Cache Key Generator
description: Create cache/keys.py with pure functions for deterministic cache key generation. fund_key(), skeleton_key(), user_index_key(), user_overlay_key(), compute_query_hash(). All functions pure, no side effects. Comprehensive type hints. Per v2 cache architecture spec section 6.2.

title: Phase 6: Merge Engine Tests
description: BDD scenarios: screener pagination correct slices, fund hydration from MGET, missing fund filtering, view merge attaches overlay to skeleton, skeleton immutability, missing overlay responses produce None. Pure function verification.

title: Phase 1: Map All SQLAlchemy Usage
description: Grep all sqlalchemy and declaro_persistum.compat imports. Document each file and function using SQLAlchemy. Create dependency graph. Determine migration order (leaf modules first). This is informational work that supports Phase 2.

title: Add input_id_override to FilterControlConfig
description: Add input_id_override field to FilterControlConfig model. Update all filter control templates (search_input, checkbox_group, multi_select, number_range, date_range, single_select) to use input_id_override when provided, falling back to generated IDs. BDD tests for each control type.
close_reason: Implemented input_id_override field in FilterControlConfig and updated all 6 filter control templates with BDD tests. All tests passing.

title: Phase 2: Convert customization persistence to Persistum
description: Rewrite customization/persistence.py using persistum query API. Replace Session.query().filter() with table().select().where(). Make all functions async. Replace SessionLocal() with ConnectionPool parameter. Remove should_close pattern.

title: Phase 1: Remove Python Formatting Package
description: Delete entire formatting/ directory (6 files), financial_integration.py, api_integration.py, domain/financial_columns.py. Remove all formatting imports from routes. Remove FormattingRequest/FormattingResponse models. Remove /tables/format endpoint. Templates already emit fx-* attributes for fmtx.js -- no Python formatting needed.

title: Phase 3: Implement Cache Warmer
description: Create cache/warmer.py. warm_tier1() for startup warming, warm_tier2_for_users() for optional pre-warm. Runs as background task (does not block startup). Reports progress. Idempotent. Per v2 spec section 2.1.

title: Phase 6: Graceful Degradation and Integration Tests
description: BDD scenarios: Redis unavailable -> Postgres fallback, Redis timeout -> fallback, Redis OOM -> LRU eviction, Postgres unavailable -> serve from cache. End-to-end: full request through two-tier cache, route -> cache -> merge -> response, warm -> hit -> response, miss -> Postgres -> cache write.

title: Phase 2: Convert Customization Models to Persistum @table
description: Rewrite customization/models.py: ColumnCustomization, UserTablePreferences, CustomizationHistory, CustomizationTemplate from SQLAlchemy ORM classes to persistum @table Pydantic models. Remove all SQLAlchemy imports. Remove to_dict() methods (use model_dump()).

title: Phase 4: Update Demo Application for v2
description: Update examples/demo/app.py: use ConnectionPool for DB, configure Redis for two-tier cache, demonstrate fund screener pagination, show cache hit vs miss paths, demonstrate graceful degradation when Redis unavailable.

title: Phase 2: Convert remaining repositories to Persistum
description: Convert customization_repository.py and preference_repository.py to persistum query API. Make all functions async with explicit ConnectionPool parameter.

title: Add totals and calculated field control types
description:
Feature: Totals and Calculated Fields
  As a developer building filter layouts
  I want to display totals and calculated values in the filter section
  So that users see summary statistics alongside filters

  Scenario: Render absolute total
    Given a FilterControlConfig with type TOTAL_ABSOLUTE
    And source_field 'market_value'
    And format 'currency'
    And label 'Total AUM'
    When the filter layout is rendered with data totaling $96M
    Then '$96M' is displayed with label 'Total AUM'

  Scenario: Render visible total (filtered data)
    Given a FilterControlConfig with type TOTAL_VISIBLE
    And source_field 'quantity'
    And format 'number'
    And label 'Positions Shown'
    When the filter layout is rendered with 150 visible rows
    Then the sum of quantity for visible rows is displayed

  Scenario: Render calculated field
    Given a FilterControlConfig with type CALCULATED_FIELD
    And formula '{reviewed_count} / {total_count} * 100'
    And format 'percentage'
    And label 'Review Progress'
    When the filter layout is rendered
    Then the calculated percentage is displayed

  Scenario: Calculated field with badge styling
    Given a CALCULATED_FIELD control
    And badge_thresholds [{min: 0, max: 50, css: 'bg-danger'}, {min: 50, max: 80, css: 'bg-warning'}, {min: 80, max: 100, css: 'bg-success'}]
    When the calculated value is 74%
    Then the badge has class 'bg-warning'

Implementation:
- Add TOTAL_ABSOLUTE, TOTAL_VISIBLE, CALCULATED_FIELD to FilterControlType enum
- Add source_field, formula, format, badge_thresholds to FilterControlConfig
- Create total_field.html and calculated_field.html templates
- Add calculation logic to rendering service
- Update filter_layout.html to include new control types
close_reason: Implemented totals and calculated field controls with passing BDD tests. Added TOTAL_ABSOLUTE, TOTAL_VISIBLE, CALCULATED_FIELD to FilterControlType enum. Added source_field, formula, format, badge_thresholds fields to FilterControlConfig. Created total_field.html and calculated_field.html templates with formatting and badge styling support. All 10 BDD scenarios passing.

title: Phase 6: Memory Budget Tests
description: Memory scenarios: Tier 1 within 13MB budget, Tier 2 per-user within 540KB, 1000 simulated users within 512MB Redis budget, LRU eviction prioritizes Tier 2 over Tier 1. Per v2 spec section 4 and 9.3.

title: Tablix Filter Enhancement Epic
description: Enhance tablix filter section with: (1) multiselect search, (2) combined render function, (3) static text/image controls, (4) totals and calculated fields. BDD/TDD approach with red tests first.
close_reason: All filter enhancement features completed with BDD/TDD approach. Delivered: (1) searchable multiselect with 5 passing tests, (2) static text/image controls with 7 passing tests, (3) totals and calculated fields with 10 passing tests, (4) render_table_ui convenience function with 5 passing tests, (5) documentation updates. Total: 27 passing BDD tests, all features implemented and documented.

title: Add static text and image filter control types
description:
Feature: Static Display Controls
  As a developer building filter layouts
  I want to add static text and images to filter sections
  So that I can create rich, branded filter UIs

  Scenario: Render static text control
    Given a FilterControlConfig with type STATIC_TEXT
    And text content 'Welcome to Holdings'
    And css_class 'text-primary fs-4'
    When the filter layout is rendered
    Then a div with the text content is displayed
    And the CSS classes are applied

  Scenario: Render static image control
    Given a FilterControlConfig with type STATIC_IMAGE
    And image_url '/assets/logo.png'
    And image_alt 'Company Logo'
    And css_class 'rounded shadow'
    When the filter layout is rendered
    Then an img element with the URL is displayed
    And the alt text is set
    And the CSS classes are applied

  Scenario: Static controls do not submit values
    Given a filter form with static text and image controls
    When the form is submitted
    Then static controls are not included in form data

Implementation:
- Add STATIC_TEXT and STATIC_IMAGE to FilterControlType enum
- Add text_content, image_url, image_alt fields to FilterControlConfig
- Create static_text.html and static_image.html templates
- Update filter_layout.html to include new control types
close_reason: Implemented static text and image filter controls with passing BDD tests. Added STATIC_TEXT and STATIC_IMAGE to FilterControlType enum, added text_content/image_url/image_alt fields to FilterControlConfig, created static_text.html and static_image.html templates. All 7 BDD scenarios passing.

title: Phase 5: Implement Introspection API
description: Programmatic introspection: get_class_names() returns dict[str, ClassInfo] with descriptions, component, tokens. get_token_contract() returns dict[str, TokenInfo] with types, defaults (light+dark), descriptions. get_component_map() maps component names to class names. All return frozen Pydantic models. Generate JSON documentation manifest alongside compiled CSS for external tooling, linters, test selectors, AI assistants.

title: Update documentation for ID generation features
description: Search all documentation files (CLAUDE.md, README.md, docstrings) and update with new ID generation features: cell_id_template, row_id_template, table_id_override, FilterLayoutConfig ID overrides, FilterControlConfig input_id_override. Include usage examples.
close_reason: Updated CLAUDE.md with comprehensive documentation for all new ID generation features, including table_id_override, row_id_template, cell_id_template, FilterLayoutConfig ID overrides, and FilterControlConfig input_id_override with usage examples.

title: Add search option to multiselect filter control
description:
Feature: Multiselect Search
  As a user filtering table data
  I want to search within multiselect dropdown options
  So that I can quickly find options in long lists

  Scenario: Search filters multiselect options
    Given a multiselect filter with 100 options
    When I type 'tech' in the search box
    Then only options containing 'tech' are visible

  Scenario: Search is case-insensitive
    Given a multiselect filter with options including 'Technology'
    When I type 'TECH' in the search box
    Then 'Technology' option is visible

  Scenario: Empty search shows all options
    Given a multiselect filter with filtered options
    When I clear the search box
    Then all options are visible again

Implementation:
- Add searchable: bool field to FilterControlConfig
- Update multi_select.html template with search input
- Add JavaScript for filtering options
close_reason: Implemented searchable multiselect with BDD tests passing

title: Phase 3: Implement Merge Engine - Screener
description: Create merge/screener.py. serve_screener_page() -- paginates cached index by slicing, hydrates from Tier 1 via MGET. Pure function (no DB, no cache writes). Handles missing fund records. Returns typed ScreenerResult. Per v2 spec section 3.3.

title: Phase 6: Cache Key Generation Tests
description: BDD test scenarios: deterministic key generation (same inputs -> same key), key format correctness, hash collision resistance, user scoping in Tier 2 keys. Unit tests for all key generation functions.

title: Phase 4: Rewrite Route Handlers for v2
description: Rewrite routes/table_routes.py: remove formatting routes, remove SQLAlchemy deps, add async ConnectionPool and Redis as FastAPI deps, integrate two-tier cache flow, integrate graceful degradation. Rewrite routes/models.py: remove formatting models, add cache status to health check.

title: Phase 3: Implement Cache Configuration
description: Create cache/config.py with frozen Pydantic model TablixCacheConfig. TTL values, Redis URLs, compression settings, connection pool config, feature flags. Factory function from env vars. Per v2 cache architecture spec section 10.1.

title: Update documentation for filter enhancements
description:
Feature: Documentation Sync
  As a developer using tablix
  I want accurate documentation for all filter features
  So that I can implement them correctly

  Scenario: CLAUDE.md is updated
    Given the filter enhancements are implemented
    When I read CLAUDE.md
    Then it documents all new FilterControlType values
    And it shows usage examples for each new control type

  Scenario: Code docstrings are complete
    Given all new functions and classes
    When I read their docstrings
    Then they explain all parameters
    And they include usage examples

  Scenario: README reflects new capabilities
    Given the tablix README.md exists
    When I read it
    Then it lists all filter control types
    And it shows the render_table_ui() convenience function

Tasks:
- Search all .md files in repo for filter-related content
- Update CLAUDE.md with new FilterControlType values
- Update any README or docs with filter examples
- Verify all docstrings are complete
- Add inline code examples where helpful
close_reason: Updated documentation with all new filter enhancements. Updated CLAUDE.md with new FilterControlType enum values (static_text, static_image, total_absolute, total_visible, calculated_field) and usage examples. Updated README.md Quick Start to use render_table_ui and documented all filter control types. All docstrings verified as complete.

title: Add ID override fields to FilterLayoutConfig
description: Add container_id_override, form_id_override, and controls_id_override fields to FilterLayoutConfig model. Update filter_layout.html template to use these IDs when provided, falling back to generated IDs otherwise. BDD tests for all three override scenarios.
close_reason: Implemented container_id_override, form_id_override, and controls_id_override fields in FilterLayoutConfig with BDD tests. All tests passing.

title: Phase 2: Convert table_config_repository to Persistum
description: Rewrite repositories/table_config_repository.py using persistum query API. Replace all text() raw SQL with table().select(), insert(), update(), delete(). Make all functions async. Replace Session with ConnectionPool parameter.

title: Tablix Row/Cell ID Generation Epic
description:
Add row and cell ID generation to Tablix templates to address 572 accessibility violations. Currently table.html generates NO IDs for <tr> or <td> elements.

Goals:
- Enable ID templates for table rows and cells
- Support ID overrides for filter layouts and controls
- Maintain backward compatibility (IDs only generated when templates provided)

BDD/TDD approach with red tests first, documentation updates at end.
close_reason: All 5 sub-tasks completed: cell_id_template, row_id_template, table_id_override, FilterLayout ID overrides, FilterControl input_id_override. 127 tests passing (53 BDD tests). Documentation updated.

title: Add cell_id_template to ColumnDefinition
description:
Feature: Cell ID Template
  As a developer building accessible tables
  I want to specify ID templates for table cells
  So that each cell has a unique, predictable ID

  Scenario: ColumnDefinition accepts cell_id_template field
    Given a ColumnDefinition model
    When I set cell_id_template to 'cell-aum-{row_idx}'
    Then the config is valid
    And config.cell_id_template equals 'cell-aum-{row_idx}'

  Scenario: cell_id_template defaults to None
    Given a ColumnDefinition without cell_id_template specified
    Then config.cell_id_template is None

  Scenario: Table renders cell IDs when template provided
    Given a TableConfig with a column having cell_id_template='cell-value-{row_idx}'
    And table data with 3 rows
    When the table is rendered
    Then cell in row 0 has id='cell-value-0'
    And cell in row 1 has id='cell-value-1'
    And cell in row 2 has id='cell-value-2'

  Scenario: No cell IDs when template not provided
    Given a TableConfig with a column without cell_id_template
    When the table is rendered
    Then cells have no id attribute

Implementation:
- Add cell_id_template: str | None field to ColumnDefinition in domain/models.py
- Update table.html template to generate cell IDs
close_reason: Implemented cell_id_template field in ColumnDefinition with BDD tests. All tests passing.

title: Phase 3: Implement Tier 2 Per-User Data Manager
description: Create cache/tier2.py. get_or_create_index(), get_or_create_overlay(), invalidate_user_index(), invalidate_user_overlay(), invalidate_all_user_data(). All async, pure functions, no global state. Optional LZ4 compression. Per v2 spec section 3.2.

title: Tablix v2: Complete Architecture Migration
description: Epic covering the full Tablix v2 migration: remove Python formatting, migrate SQLAlchemy to persistum, implement two-tier cache architecture, and CSS design token system. Removal-first strategy.

title: Add convenience function to combine header + filters + table
description:
Feature: Combined Table Rendering
  As a developer using tablix
  I want a single function to render header, filters, and table together
  So that I can easily create complete table UIs

  Scenario: Render complete table UI with all sections
    Given a TableConfig with header configuration
    And a FilterLayoutConfig with filter controls
    And TableData with rows
    When I call render_table_ui()
    Then the output contains the header section
    And the output contains the filter section
    And the output contains the table section

  Scenario: Render without header
    Given a TableConfig without header configuration
    When I call render_table_ui()
    Then the output does not contain a header section
    And the output contains the filter and table sections

  Scenario: Render without filters
    Given a TableConfig with header
    And no FilterLayoutConfig
    When I call render_table_ui()
    Then the output contains header and table only

Implementation:
- Add render_table_ui() function to rendering_service.py
- Accept optional header, filters, table_state parameters
- Export from services __init__.py
close_reason: Implemented render_table_ui convenience function with passing BDD tests. Added function to templates/__init__.py that combines header, filters, and table rendering into a single call. Supports optional header and filters sections. All 5 BDD scenarios passing.

title: Add row_id_template and table_id_override to TableConfig
description:
Feature: Row and Table ID Templates
  As a developer building accessible tables
  I want to specify ID templates for table rows and override table IDs
  So that each row has a unique ID and tables can be targeted by specific IDs

  Scenario: TableConfig accepts row_id_template field
    Given a TableConfig model
    When I set row_id_template to 'holding-row-{row_idx}'
    Then the config is valid
    And config.row_id_template equals 'holding-row-{row_idx}'

  Scenario: TableConfig accepts table_id_override field
    Given a TableConfig model
    When I set table_id_override to 'my-custom-table'
    Then the config is valid
    And config.table_id_override equals 'my-custom-table'

  Scenario: row_id_template defaults to None
    Given a TableConfig without row_id_template specified
    Then config.row_id_template is None

  Scenario: Table renders row IDs when template provided
    Given a TableConfig with row_id_template='row-{row_idx}'
    And table data with 3 rows
    When the table is rendered
    Then row 0 has id='row-0'
    And row 1 has id='row-1'
    And row 2 has id='row-2'

  Scenario: Table uses override ID when provided
    Given a TableConfig with table_id_override='custom-holdings'
    When the table is rendered
    Then the table element has id='custom-holdings'

  Scenario: Table uses default ID when no override
    Given a TableConfig with table_name='holdings' and no table_id_override
    When the table is rendered
    Then the table element has id='tablix-holdings'

Implementation:
- Add row_id_template: str | None to TableConfig
- Add table_id_override: str | None to TableConfig
- Update table.html to use override ID and generate row IDs
close_reason: Implemented row_id_template and table_id_override fields in TableConfig with BDD tests. All tests passing.

title: Phase 1: Remove Old Caching Infrastructure
description: Delete caching/cache_service.py, cache_invalidation.py, cache_middleware.py, caching/__init__.py, repositories/cache_repository.py. Remove cache-related imports and endpoints from routes. Remove CacheRequest/CacheResponse from route models.

title: Phase 5: Define Design Token Architecture
description: Design the complete token system. NAMING: Hybrid B+C — ~30-40 public semantic tokens (--tablix-color-primary, --tablix-spacing-gap) as the contract, component-level opt-in overrides via var(--tablix-search-radius, var(--tablix-radius)) fallback. SCHEMA: Token config in YAML with light/dark pairs for colors. Python dict input via TypedDict. Single-source enforcement (YAML OR dict, not both). THREE MODES: themed (full visual with light-dark() colors), minimal (structural + spacing), headless (structural only). DARK MODE: All colors use CSS light-dark() notation. color-scheme: light dark on :root. No @media prefers-color-scheme. No .dark class. No a11y @media (genX handles it). SCOPE: All components — table, filters, pagination, headers. CLASS NAMES: tablix-{component}-{element} convention.

title: Phase 2: Remove SQLAlchemy dependency from pyproject.toml
description: Final grep for any remaining sqlalchemy imports (should be zero). Remove sqlalchemy>=2.0.0 from pyproject.toml dependencies. Run uv sync. Run full test suite. Blocks on all other Phase 2 tasks.

title: Phase 5: Implement Inline Style Override System
description: Add style override dict to render_table_ui(). Consumer passes styles={'.tablix-search-field': {'border-radius': '999px'}, '#filter-id': {'width': '400px'}}. style_mode switch: 'inline' emits <style> block before HTML, 'class' generates named override classes (tablix-override-{hash}) written to compiled CSS and applied to elements. All elements must have IDs for targetability. Complements token system for per-instance overrides.

title: Phase 6: Tier 1 and Tier 2 Cache Tests
description: BDD scenarios: fund cache warm loads all records, single fund lookup, MGET page hydration, missing fund handling, TTL expiry, invalidation. Index creation on miss, cache hit, overlay creation, user isolation, TTL expiry, user write invalidation.

title: Phase 3: Implement Invalidation Handler
description: Create cache/invalidation.py. invalidate_on_user_write() (Tier 2 only), invalidate_on_data_refresh() (Tier 1 funds), invalidate_on_regulatory_change() (Tier 1 skeleton). Fire-and-forget safe. Tier 1 and Tier 2 independently invalidated. Per v2 spec section 3.4.

title: Phase 3: Implement Tier 1 Shared Data Manager
description: Create cache/tier1.py. warm_fund_cache(), get_fund_record(), get_fund_records() (MGET), warm_skeleton(), get_skeleton(), invalidate_funds(), invalidate_skeleton(). All async, pure functions, no global state. Uses orjson serialization and persistum ConnectionPool. Per v2 spec section 3.1.

title: Phase 3: Implement Graceful Degradation
description: Add degradation logic across cache modules. Redis connection failure -> PostgreSQL fallback. Redis timeout -> PostgreSQL fallback. Merge error -> direct PostgreSQL result. Circuit breaker for repeated Redis failures. Logging at each level. Per v2 spec section 8.

title: Phase 2: Convert table_data_repository to Persistum
description: Rewrite repositories/table_data_repository.py using persistum query API. Replace Session with ConnectionPool. Replace text() SQL with table().select().where().execute(). Make all functions async. Replace information_schema queries with persistum inspector. Remove all SQLAlchemy imports.

title: Phase 3: Implement Merge Engine - Views
description: Create merge/views.py. merge_view(skeleton, overlay) -> dict. Pure function (no side effects, no DB access). Deep copies skeleton. Walks nested tree attaching overlay at leaf nodes. Returns new merged tree. Per v2 spec section 3.3.
